clues: This substance is naturally produced by some plants in the nightshade family and very small, harmless amounts can be found in common foods such as tomatoes, potatoes, and eggplants. The leaves of some of these plants have been used in indigenous communities for ceremonial, recreational, and medicinal purposes
Although you might think otherwise, this highly addictive substance is not classified as a carcinogen
When inhaled, this substance reaches the brain in just 10-20 seconds and can modulate reward systems. Other delivery systems include patches, gum, or pouches

Today, 'hair of the dog' is an idiom referring to the consumption of alcohol to cure a hangover. However, in medieval Europe, it was a folklore remedy where Pliny the Elder prescribed placing the charred hair of a dog affected with this disease into the open wound as a cure.
Due to the erratic and aggressive behavior historically seen in affected individuals, the origin of this disease's name likely comes from an ancient Sanskrit word meaning 'to do violence'
Only mammals are affected by this virus, which paralyzes the throat leading to a heavy buildup of saliva. Drinking causes painful muscle spasms
Nalidixic acid was the very first in this class, an accidental byproduct in the synthesis of the antimalarial drug chloroquine. Later addition of a fluorine atom really expanded usage
One of this class of medications, which work by targeting DNA gyrase and topoisomerase IV, was famously stockpiled during the 2001 anthrax attacks to treat inhaled anthrax
Because these antibiotics penetrate tissue so deeply, they can occasionally disrupt natural connective tissues and nerves, like achilles tendons

After WWII, there was a large surplus of hydrazine, a key rocket component so it was sold at reduced prices and pharmaceutical agencies used this to synthesize iproniazid as a tuberculosis treatment. They incidentally discovered other properties leading to iproniazid being classified as the first in this medication class

Dr. Hannibal Lecter's line 'I ate his liver with some fava beans and a nice Chianti' in the Silence of the Lambs, subtly suggests he is not taking his actively prescribed medications as patients taking this class of medications should avoid consuming foods high in tyramine, which can lead to severe hypertensive crises
Because of the food interactions and risk of serotonin syndrome, these medications are typically reserved for refractory cases or specific symptoms such as rejection sensitivity

Did you know that Alexander Hamilton had an affair, which was the first major sex scandal in US political history? His son, William Stephen Hamilton, came to California during the Gold Rush to escape this scandal but unfortunately, William developed this disease and died in Sacramento, CA in 1950
The name of the causal bacteria comes from Latin meaning 'to quiver'. The microbe has a whip-like tail that allows it to swim at astonishing speeds and change directions in milliseconds

When playing the Oregon Trail, you might be told that 'you have died of dysentery'. On the real Oregon trail, this similar disease was actually the single deadliest killer triggering extreme dehydration and could kill healthy adults in just a few hours because patients can excrete up to 1 liter of rice-water stool per hour
